methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sage Geosystems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Cindy Taff and Lance Cook, currently valued at $400M post-money with $114M+ in cumulative capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of five same-sector, same-stage companies ranging from $120M to $5.0B, plus one public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $400M base valuation, implying $760M. The peer-median method references the median valuation across five Series B climate and energy peers—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M)—which yields a median of $310M. The wide peer range ($120M–$5.0B) reflects material heterogeneity in capital intensity and technology risk within the sector.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $264M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $535M. The current $400M valuation falls within this range but toward the lower end, suggesting modest upside on a stage-basis but sits above peer median, implying a premium to comparable companies. Key uncertainties include the applicability of stage-progression multipliers given the wide peer dispersion and Sage's positioning relative to capital requirements and technology maturity within climate tech. Peer comparability remains constrained by heterogeneous business models and development timelines.
